verses.2: “Do you think this is in accordance with justice? Do you say, ‘My righteousness is more than God’s’?
verses.3: “For you say, ‘What advantage will it be to You? What benefit will I have, more than if I had sinned?’
verses.4: “I will answer you, And your friends with you.
verses.5: “Look at the heavens and see; And look at the clouds—they are higher than you.
verses.6: “If you have sinned, what do you accomplish against Him? And if your wrongdoings are many, what do you do to Him?
verses.7: “If you are righteous, what do you give to Him, Or what does He receive from your hand?
verses.8: “Your wickedness is for a man like yourself, And your righteousness is for a son of man.
verses.9: “Because of the multitude of oppressions they cry out; They cry for help because of the arm of the mighty.
verses.10: “But no one says, ‘Where is God my Maker, Who gives songs in the night,
verses.11: Who teaches us more than the animals of the earth And makes us wiser than the birds of the sky?’
verses.12: “There they cry out, but He does not answer Because of the pride of evil people.
verses.13: “God certainly will not listen to an empty cry, Nor will the Almighty regard it.
verses.14: “How much less when you say you do not look at Him, The case is before Him, and you must wait for Him!
verses.15: “And now, because He has not avenged His anger, Nor has He acknowledged wrongdoing well,
verses.16: So Job opens his mouth with empty words; He multiplies words without knowledge.”
